items.title: Jai Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure
items.summary: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

items.title: Family's story of dementia shines spotlight on Australia's leading cause of death
items.summary: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

items.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction in Adelaide as SA party opposes fracking proposal
items.summary: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' absence from Aboriginal housing and homelands conference as 'disgraceful'
items.summary: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

items.title: Bus carrying school students crashes into tree in Western Sydney's Erskine Park
items.summary: All students have been taken off the bus and were assessed by paramedics, Fire and Rescue New South Wales said.

items.title: Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.15L of wine before fatal hit-run gets reduced jail term on appeal
items.summary: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.

items.title: Kylie doco is a human portrait of pop royalty's resilience and reinvention
items.summary: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
items.summary: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

items.title: Australia's largest diphtheria outbreak prompts $7.2m federal vaccine blitz
items.summary: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

items.title: Football Australia posts record losses as plan to axe 20 per cent of staff revealed
items.summary: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

items.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days
items.summary: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.

items.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the most important satirists in American history
items.summary: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

items.title: Unemployment rate jumps to 4.5 per cent, driven by female unemployment
items.summary: All four major banks now expect the RBA to keep the cash rate on hold at its next meeting on June 16, as the unemployment rate spikes to 4.5 per cent.

items.title: $3.5 million support package as Great Western Highway closure drags on
items.summary: The NSW government announces $3.5 million for communities that have lost visitors and revenue after part of the Great Western Highway was unexpectedly closed.

items.title: Tech giant Nvidia reports growth of 85 per cent due to the construction of AI factories
items.summary: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

items.title: On the eve of Stephen Colbert's final Late Show, America is asking: Can late night survive the Trump administration?
items.summary: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns
items.summary: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

items.title: Birth choices dwindle as new laws push Geraldton's last private midwife to press pause in WA
items.summary: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

items.title: Prince William goes wild as Aston Villa claims Europa League title and ends 30-year drought
items.summary: In front of a joyful future king of England, Aston Villa coach Unai Emery reaffirmed his status as the king of the Europa League as Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Istanbul to secure Emery a record-extending fifth title in the Europa League.

items.title: What does broken heart syndrome feel like?
items.summary: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

items.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects
items.summary: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

items.title: Elderly woman dies 11 days after fatal crash on Queensland's Southern Downs
items.summary: The woman has died of injuries sustained as a passenger in an SUV that allegedly swerved to avoid an oncoming car in the wrong lane on May 9.

items.title: Retiring NRL star Jai Arrow says he will fight after shock MND diagnosis
items.summary: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

items.title: Abrolhos Islands coral reef shows resistance to climate bleaching, study finds
items.summary: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

items.title: Congo football team cancels Kinshasa World Cup farewell as Ebola outbreak concerns grow
items.summary: The Congo football team cancels its farewell in Kinshasa as the US clarified what the rules would be for players and staff entering the country for the World Cup.

items.title: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing will pay more tax under CGT changes
items.summary: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

items.title: Iran examining US proposal as Donald Trump says talks in 'final stages'
items.summary: Iran says it is examining the latest US proposal received from mediator Pakistan in talks to end the Middle East war, despite the two sides trading threats over resuming attacks.

items.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities
items.summary: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

items.title: Australian rugby 7s co-captain Isabella Nasser inspired by 2016 Olympics victory
items.summary: When Isabella Nasser tuned in to watch the 2016 Rio Olympics as a 13-year-old, she had little idea of the pathway her life was about to take.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask
items.summary: ACT industry groups say drug use and widespread construction are among issues eroding feelings of safety.

items.title: Strathmerton fears it will become 'ghost town' as Bega Cheese factory closure nears
items.summary: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

items.title: Scott Pendlebury's 433rd game simply the latest in a long line of Collingwood star's AFL records
items.summary: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.

items.title: A year on from record floods, NSW Mid North Coast residents are returning home
items.summary: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mid North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

items.title: Critics argue government's response to aged care price caps 'a total joke'
items.summary: Some advocates say the government's decision to delay price caps for at-home aged care services is a "burden".

items.title: Calls to support struggling parents as national survey reveals increasing psychological distress
items.summary: As the birth rate hits a record low it's been revealed that almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national parenting survey.

items.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID
items.summary: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.

items.title: Fear in Karumba fishing industry after shock closure as locals wonder what's on horizon
items.summary: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".

items.title: New research finds gentoo penguins are four distinct species
items.summary: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

items.title: 'Short-term pain' to wrestle Tasmanian budget back to black in two years, Abetz says
items.summary: There will be "short-term pain" to get Tasmania's finances back in the black within two years, state treasurer Eric Abetz says ahead of today's budget.

items.title: Political cost of budget tax plan emerges as Labor focuses on future
items.summary: In an already difficult post-budget week, Labor isn't being helped by some of its "friends". But it has won an important endorsement from one powerful voice.

items.title: BOM winter forecast predicts unusually warm season as El Niño increasingly likely
items.summary: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

items.title: The majority of Tuvalu has applied to relocate to Australia to escape climate change. What happens now?
items.summary: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.

items.title: Pressure builds on new-home industry as Iran war sends construction costs through the roof
items.summary: Amid fast-rising prices — from materials craned in, to rubbish trucked out — a residential builder lists the costlier surcharges, levies and products hammering the construction industry and fuelling inflation.

items.title: Can The Mandalorian and Grogu save Star Wars?
items.summary: Grogu (aka Baby Yoda) made The Mandalorian a hit on streaming, now Disney is looking to parlay that goodwill into ticket sales to the latest Star Wars cinematic release.

items.title: Music quiz: Can you pick the classical music in these iconic movies?
items.summary: From Mozart to Max Richter, can you pick the classical music in these iconic films?

items.title: Why renovating your outdoor area gives 'the best bang for your buck'
items.summary: Adding a new kitchen or bathroom to your home can be expensive, but budget constraints don't mean you have to take home improvement off the table altogether.

items.title: Diphtheria outbreak spreads in Australia
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Should startup businesses be exempt from capital gains tax?
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Vladimir Putin meets 'old friend' Xi Jinping in Beijing and secures more than 20 agreements
items.summary: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.

items.title: Third attempt to change SA's abortion laws since 2024 goes before state parliament
items.summary: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

items.title: Queensland's new public hospital to refuse voluntary assisted dying care, abortions and contraception for religious reasons
items.summary: The Queensland premier has opened the state's first major new public hospital in almost a decade — at Springfield, south-west of Brisbane — but it won't offer some services for religious reasons.

items.title: San Diego white supremacist shooters named Australia's worst mass murderer in online posts
items.summary: Thousands of kilometres away, but the terrible connection to Australia is evident. ABC NEWS Verify looks at the inspiration behind the San Diego Islamic Centre murders.

items.title: Health officials warn diphtheria cases could rise in biggest outbreak on record
items.summary: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

items.title: Carlos Alcaraz rules himself out of Wimbledon, entire grass-court swing, with wrist injury
items.summary: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

items.title: NSW Auditor-General finds flaws in Australia's costliest disaster recovery program
items.summary: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

items.title: Queensland hit by triple injury blow for State of Origin III as Blues look for rare series whitewash
items.summary: Injuries to three key players have compounded Queensland's task to avoid a 3-0 women's State of Origin whitewash.

items.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about the disease
items.summary: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.

items.title: Visy recycling plant still leaves smell over Reservoir after years of complaints
items.summary: A urban planning expert says Victoria's industrial zoning laws are outdated and that operations like Visy's paper recycling plant in Reservoir may no longer be appropriate so close to housing.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rebounds above $4,000 per tonne
items.summary: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

items.title: Jai Arrow, South Sydney and Queensland Maroons star, retires from NRL due to motor neurone disease
items.summary: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

items.title: Marine species encountered on Ocean Census expeditions
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Ocean Census project discovers 1,121 new species of ocean life around the world in one year
items.summary: Thousands of species have been discovered lurking and thriving in our oceans, as part of an effort to catalogue 100,000 new species.

items.title: Ocean Census discovers over 1,100 new marine species
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Federal government prepares diphtheria support package after more than 220 cases reported
items.summary: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

items.title: One dead after multi-vehicle crash near Victorian-New South Wales border town of Mildura
items.summary: NSW Police say a van had rear-ended a vehicle, causing the vehicle to collide with a B-double truck travelling the opposite way.
